info.title: PayID Platform API
info.description: API documentation for the PayID Platform — including OAuth 2.0 Authentication, Organization Management, API Keys, KYC Verification, Analytics, Billing, and more.

apiKeyAuth.name: Authorization
apiKeyAuth.description: API Key via Authorization header. Format: `ApiKey <key>`

xApiKeyAuth.name: X-Api-Key
xApiKeyAuth.description: API Key via X-Api-Key header. Format: `<key>` (no prefix needed)

post.description: Authenticate with email and password. Returns an access token and optional refresh/ID tokens.
Create a new user account and immediately log in. Returns an access token.
Send a one-click login link to the user's email address.

get.description: Verify a magic link token sent via email and exchange it for an access token.

post.description: Send a one-time password (OTP) to the user's email or phone number.
Verify a one-time password and exchange it for an access token.

get.description: Redirect the user to Google's OAuth 2.0 authorization page.
Handle the callback from Google OAuth and return access tokens.
Check the status of the current session and return the authenticated user's details.

post.description: Invalidate the current session and revoke associated tokens.

get.description: Initiate an OAuth 2.0 authorization flow. Validates the request parameters and redirects the user to the consent screen.

post.description: Submit the OAuth 2.0 authorization consent decision and redirect to the client's redirect URI with a code or token.
Exchange an authorization code, client credentials, or refresh token for an access token.
Revoke an OAuth 2.0 access token or refresh token, making it immediately invalid.
Inspect the active state and metadata of an OAuth 2.0 access or refresh token.

get.description: Return OpenID Connect claims about the currently authenticated end-user.
Retrieve the OpenID Connect discovery document with endpoint URLs and supported features.
Retrieve the public keys used to verify JWT access tokens and ID tokens.

post.description: Create a new organization. The authenticated user becomes the owner.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated list of all organizations the authenticated user belongs to.
Retrieve details of a specific organization by its ID.

patch.description: Update an organization's name, settings, or metadata.

delete.description: Permanently delete an organization and all associated data. This action cannot be undone.

post.description: Transfer organization ownership to another existing member.
Suspend an organization, disabling access for all members. Requires platform admin or owner permission.
Reactivate a previously suspended organization.

get.description: Export all organization data as a structured JSON payload for compliance, backup, or migration purposes.

post.description: Send an email invitation to a user to join the organization with a specified role.

get.description: Retrieve all active members and pending invitations for an organization, with optional role filtering.

patch.description: Change a member's role within the organization.

delete.description: Remove a member from the organization. The member loses all access immediately.

post.description: Accept a membership invitation and join the organization.
Decline a membership invitation.

get.description: Retrieve the details of a pending membership invitation by its token.

post.description: Register a new OAuth 2.0 client application for the organization.

get.description: Retrieve all OAuth clients registered for an organization.
Retrieve the full configuration of a specific OAuth client.

patch.description: Update an OAuth client's configuration, scopes, redirect URIs, or branding.

delete.description: Delete an OAuth client and revoke all tokens associated with it.

post.description: Generate a new client secret and immediately invalidate the previous one.

get.description: Retrieve usage statistics and token issuance counts for a specific OAuth client.
Retrieve public-facing information about an OAuth client, used for rendering consent screens.

post.description: Create a new API key for the organization. The full key value is only returned once — store it securely.

get.description: Retrieve all API keys for an organization, with optional filtering by revocation status.
Retrieve details of a specific API key by its ID.

delete.description: Permanently revoke an API key. Any requests using this key will be rejected immediately.

get.description: Retrieve the organization's configuration including CORS origins, token TTLs, webhook settings, and rate limits.

patch.description: Update the organization's CORS origins, token TTLs, webhook URL, or rate limits.

get.description: Retrieve API call counts, token issuance, and active user statistics for an organization over a time range.
Retrieve the monthly active user (MAU) count for an organization for a given month.
Retrieve error rates, error codes, and failure counts for an organization over a time range.
Retrieve API response latency statistics (p50, p95, p99) for an organization over a time range.
Retrieve a filtered, paginated list of audit events for an organization. Filter by event type, actor, or date range.
Retrieve the full details of a specific audit log event.
Retrieve the organization's current billing information, plan tier, and payment details.

patch.description: Update billing email, company name, address, or payment method for the organization.

post.description: Upgrade the organization to a higher billing tier to unlock additional features and higher limits.
Downgrade the organization to a lower billing tier.
Confirm an email address using the verification token sent by email.

get.description: Look up a user by their unique PayID pay tag.
Retrieve all KYC verification records for a specific user (admin or platform use).
Retrieve all active OAuth consent grants the current user has given to third-party applications.
Retrieve the details of a specific OAuth consent grant.

delete.description: Revoke a specific OAuth consent grant. The associated application will lose access immediately.

get.description: Retrieve the profile and account details of the currently authenticated user.

patch.description: Update the authenticated user's profile information such as display name or pay tag.

post.description: Send an email verification link to the current user's email address.

get.description: Retrieve all KYC verification records associated with the current user.

post.description: Start a new KYC identity verification session using the configured provider.

get.description: Check the current status of a KYC verification session. Pass `refresh=true` to force-fetch the latest status from the provider.

post.description: Retry a failed KYC verification session, optionally switching to a different provider.

get.description: Retrieve a paginated, filtered list of KYC verification sessions for the current user.

post.description: Receive and process webhook events from the Plaid KYC provider.
Receive and process webhook events from the QoreID KYC provider.

get.description: Retrieve the KYC provider configuration for an organization, including selected provider and verification settings.

patch.description: Update the KYC provider configuration for an organization.

get.description: Retrieve all KYC verification sessions associated with an organization.
Retrieve KYC verification pass/fail rates and volume statistics for an organization.

post.description: Create a new verification intent that guides a user through the identity verification process.
Redeem a hosted verification link token to retrieve the associated intent and start a session.

get.description: Retrieve the current state and details of a verification intent by its ID.
Retrieve all verification intents belonging to the currently authenticated user.

patch.description: Update the current step, draft data, or metadata of a verification intent as the user progresses through the flow.

post.description: Start a third-party identity provider session for a verification intent. Returns provider-specific session data.
Cancel a pending or in-progress verification intent.
Resolve the return URL for a verification intent after the user completes or exits the provider flow.
